note: Declarative theorem -> 01e-property mapping (Stage C of the impl plan). Tune these entries to match the fusaka 01e benchmark granularity WITHOUT recompiling Lean. `theorem` must be the fully-qualified Lean name so `speca-export` can resolve it; `covers_hint` is matched against 01b subgraph element ids by the driver; `bug_bounty_area` is checked against BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json.

properties.text: A validator MUST NOT sign two attestations with the same target epoch for distinct targets (equivocation).
properties.assertion: For every validator v and target epoch e, at most one target block-root is signed by v. Two distinct signed targets at the same target epoch witness slashable equivocation (Casper FFG condition S1).
properties.exploitability: Requires a validator signing key. A client that fails to detect S1 equivocation lets a Byzantine validator commit to two histories without being slashed, weakening accountable safety.

properties.text: A validator MUST NOT cast an attestation whose (source, target) span strictly surrounds, or is surrounded by, another of its attestations (surround vote).
properties.assertion: For every validator v, there are no two attestations a1, a2 by v such that source(a1) < source(a2) and target(a2) < target(a1). Such a pair witnesses slashable surround voting (Casper FFG condition S2).
properties.exploitability: Requires a validator signing key. Missing S2 detection permits surround voting that can finalize conflicting checkpoints.

properties.text: If two conflicting checkpoints are both finalized, a ⅔ quorum intersection MUST be provably slashable.
properties.assertion: finalization-fork(state) implies q-intersection-slashed(state): every validator in the intersection of the two supporting ⅔ quorums has violated S1 or S2. A client MUST NOT finalize two conflicting checkpoints absent detectable slashing.
properties.exploitability: A safety violation (two conflicting finalized checkpoints) is the strongest consensus failure. This property is the accountability guarantee; a client bug that finalizes a fork without the slashable-intersection invariant breaks it.

properties.text: The ⅔-quorum intersection forced to be slashed MUST have positive weight, accounting for validator activation/exit churn.
properties.assertion: The slashable quorum intersection weight is bounded below by max(wt(V_L)-a_L-e_R, wt(V_R)-a_R-e_L) - f_{1/3}(wt(V_L)) - f_{1/3}(wt(V_R)) > 0. Quorum weight arithmetic MUST NOT under/overflow or double-count churned validators.
properties.exploitability: Incorrect quorum-weight arithmetic (overflow, churn double-count) can misjudge whether a ⅔ threshold is met, affecting justification/finalization decisions.

properties.text: A checkpoint is justified iff there is a bounded justification link chain of supermajority links from the genesis/anchor.
properties.assertion: justified(c) holds iff a finite chain of ⅔-supermajority-linked checkpoints reaches c from the anchor. Justification-status computation MUST match this bounded-chain characterization (no unbounded recursion, no off-by-one on the height gap).
properties.exploitability: A justification computation that diverges from the bounded-chain rule can justify/finalize incorrectly, but requires control over checkpoint structure rather than a single crafted message.

properties.text: While ⅔ of stake is honest, it is always possible to extend the chain and finalize without introducing new slashing.
properties.assertion: If ≥⅔ of stake is unslashed/honest then a good quorum exists at each step, so the protocol can always extend to finalize a new checkpoint without any honest validator becoming slashable (plausible liveness).
properties.exploitability: Liveness property. Typically outside a client bug-bounty's safety scope; flagged bug_bounty_eligible=false unless the scope explicitly includes liveness.
